name: Ruby Storm
macro_plan: A dedicated spells-combo deck. It wants to deploy one or two cost reducers, chain rituals and cheap cantrips to build a high storm count and refill, then end the game with Grapeshot (or a Wish target). Ral, Monsoon Mage is both a reducer and a win condition once transformed. The deck is fragile to disruption and racing, so it leans on Wish/protection to find the missing piece and resolve the kill on turns 3-5.

mana_reducers.notes: The engine. One reducer makes rituals net positive; two makes the deck explode. Ral transforms after enough spells and becomes a finisher too.

rituals.notes: Manamorphose is only a net-positive ritual with a reducer down. Past in Flames flashes back the whole graveyard of rituals/cantrips for a second wind.

card_advantage.notes: 'Exile the top two, play until end of next turn' impulse-draw effects. There are several near-identical versions; they keep the chain going and find the kill.

dig_draw.notes: Cheap cantrips and impulse draw that also pad storm count.

tutors_wildcards.notes: Wish is a wildcard: it can fetch Grapeshot, a counter, removal, or whatever the situation needs from the sideboard. Treat as 'could be anything'.

win_conditions.notes: Grapeshot scales with storm. Transformed Ral is a recurring burst. Wish can grab the finisher.

threats.notes: Almost no creature threats; the planeswalker side of Ral is the closest thing to a beater.

removal.notes: Mostly out of the sideboard / splash; rarely maindeck removal.

counterspells.notes: Cheap protection to push the combo through, usually sideboarded in.

protection.notes: Cards that protect the combo turn from counterspells or discard.

graveyard_enablers.notes: Every cheap spell cast fills the yard for Past in Flames flashback.

recursion.notes: Past in Flames rebuys the ritual/cantrip shell; Ral replays key instants/sorceries.

engines.notes: Pieces that turn a pile of cheap spells into a kill.

payoff_cards.notes: The things the storm count is being built toward.

combo_pieces.notes: Reducer + Grapeshot is the cleanest kill; Past in Flames + a graveyard of rituals is the backup engine.

hate_pieces.notes: Sideboard answers to opposing hate (Rest in Peace, counterspells).

mana_fixing.notes: Fast lands and shocks fixing the W/U/R mana; Manamorphose also fixes.

lands.notes: Low land count (~16-18) because the deck is built to win off few lands plus rituals.

planeswalker_threats.notes: Ral is the only walker; it transforms from the front (reducer) side.

dual_role_cards.notes: Front side reduces costs; transforms into a finisher that replays spells.
Free cantrip+ritual with a reducer; fixes colors and pads storm.
Flashes back the whole graveyard of cheap spells for a second combo turn.
Wildcard tutor; can be the finisher or any answer.

tells: multiple rituals chained in one turn
Ruby Medallion / Goblin Electromancer on an early turn
impulse-draw spells (Wrenn's Resolve, Light Up the Stage)

predicted_lines.trigger: A cost reducer is on the battlefield, 4+ cards in hand, RR+ available
predicted_lines.line: Chain rituals + impulse draws to build storm, then Grapeshot for lethal or transform Ral.

predicted_lines.trigger: Past in Flames castable and graveyard full of rituals/cantrips
predicted_lines.line: Flashback the graveyard to rebuild storm count and kill.

predicted_lines.trigger: Holding up mana with Wish available
predicted_lines.line: Wish for the missing piece (finisher, counter, or removal) at instant speed.
